---
title:  
    "Simulation for effective population size estimation"
date: 
    "June 30th, 2024"
author:  
    "Ali Movasati"  
output:  
    html_document:
        #css: style.css
        toc: true
        toc_depth: 2
        toc_float: true
        number_sections: true
        theme: lumen
        highlight: zenburn
        code_folding: hide
        code_download: true # to make the Rmd source code available
---

```{r}
# Function to simulate allele frequency changes and estimate Ne
simulate_ne <- function(true_ne, 
                       initial_freq,
                       sample_size,
                       num_generations,
                       num_loci = 1,
                       replicates = 100,
                       ploidy = 2) {  # Added ploidy parameter
  
  # Initialize storage
  observed_freqs <- matrix(NA, nrow = replicates, ncol = num_loci)
  estimated_ne <- numeric(replicates)
  
  # Total number of gene copies in the population
  total_copies <- ploidy * true_ne
  
  for(rep in 1:replicates) {
    # Simulate allele frequency changes due to drift
    current_freq <- initial_freq
    
    for(gen in 1:num_generations) {
      # Binomial sampling to simulate drift
      current_freq <- rbinom(num_loci, total_copies, current_freq) / total_copies
    }
    
    # Sample individuals at final time point
    observed_count <- rbinom(num_loci, ploidy*sample_size, current_freq)
    observed_freqs[rep,] <- observed_count / (ploidy*sample_size)
    
    # Estimate Ne using temporal method
    fc <- var(observed_freqs[rep,] - initial_freq)
    
    # Adjust Ne estimation based on ploidy
    estimated_ne[rep] <- 1 / (ploidy * fc) * num_generations
  }
  
  return(list(
    true_ne = true_ne,
    ploidy = ploidy,
    estimated_ne = estimated_ne,
    mean_estimate = mean(estimated_ne),
    ci_lower = quantile(estimated_ne, 0.025),
    ci_upper = quantile(estimated_ne, 0.975)
  ))
}

simulate_ne_bottleneck <- function(true_ne, 
                                 initial_freq,
                                 sample_size,
                                 num_generations,
                                 bottleneck_proportion = 0.0005, # 0.05%
                                 bottleneck_interval = 3,
                                 num_loci = 1,
                                 replicates = 100,
                                 ploidy = 2) {
  
  observed_freqs <- matrix(NA, nrow = replicates, ncol = num_loci)
  estimated_ne <- numeric(replicates)
  
  for(rep in 1:replicates) {
    current_freq <- initial_freq
    current_ne <- true_ne
    
    for(gen in 1:num_generations) {
      # Check if it's a bottleneck generation
      if(gen %% bottleneck_interval == 0) {
        # Apply bottleneck
        current_ne <- true_ne * bottleneck_proportion
      } else {
        # Return to normal population size
        current_ne <- true_ne
      }
      
      # Simulate drift with current population size
      total_copies <- ploidy * current_ne
      current_freq <- rbinom(num_loci, total_copies, current_freq) / total_copies
    }
    
    # Sample at final time point
    observed_count <- rbinom(num_loci, ploidy*sample_size, current_freq)
    observed_freqs[rep,] <- observed_count / (ploidy*sample_size)
    
    # Estimate Ne
    fc <- var(observed_freqs[rep,] - initial_freq)
    estimated_ne[rep] <- 1 / (ploidy * fc) * num_generations
  }
  
  return(list(
    true_ne = true_ne,
    ploidy = ploidy,
    bottleneck_size = true_ne * bottleneck_proportion,
    estimated_ne = estimated_ne,
    mean_estimate = mean(estimated_ne),
    ci_lower = quantile(estimated_ne, 0.025),
    ci_upper = quantile(estimated_ne, 0.975)
  ))
}

# Compare results with and without bottleneck
set.seed(123)

# Without bottleneck (original)
normal_results <- simulate_ne(
  true_ne = 1000,
  initial_freq = 0.5,
  sample_size = 100,
  num_generations = 10,
  num_loci = 20,
  ploidy = 2
)

# With bottleneck
bottleneck_results <- simulate_ne_bottleneck(
  true_ne = 1000,
  initial_freq = 0.5,
  sample_size = 100,
  num_generations = 10,
  num_loci = 20,
  ploidy = 2
)

# Compare results
cat("Normal population:\n")
cat("True Ne:", normal_results$true_ne, "\n")
cat("Mean estimated Ne:", round(normal_results$mean_estimate), "\n")
cat("95% CI:", round(normal_results$ci_lower), "-", round(normal_results$ci_upper), "\n\n")

cat("Population with bottlenecks:\n")
cat("True Ne:", bottleneck_results$true_ne, "\n")
cat("Bottleneck size:", round(bottleneck_results$bottleneck_size), "\n")
cat("Mean estimated Ne:", round(bottleneck_results$mean_estimate), "\n")
cat("95% CI:", round(bottleneck_results$ci_lower), "-", round(bottleneck_results$ci_upper), "\n")

# Plot comparison
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)

plot_data <- data.frame(
  Normal = normal_results$estimated_ne,
  Bottleneck = bottleneck_results$estimated_ne
) %>%
  gather(key = "Type", value = "Ne")

ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Ne, fill = Type)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Ne Estimates",
       x = "Estimated Ne",
       y = "Density") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1000, linetype = "dashed", color = "red")
```

estimated_ne[rep] <- 1/(2*(1-(1-(fc/(initial_freq*(1-initial_freq))))^(1/num_generations)))
